generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 temperature sensor placed inside the insulated container to monitor internal temperature with 0.5°C resolution. Part of the cyber-physical feedback loop for DT calibration and control decisions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision DS18S20 temperature sensor placed inside the insulated container to monitor internal temperature with 0.5°C resolution. Spatial distribution designed to capture thermal gradients within the container."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring ambient conditions outside the insulated container. Provides environmental context for thermal model calibration and control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance ActuatorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedActuator
]

instance FanActuatorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FanActuator
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model with two free parameters for predicting temperature dynamics in the incubator. Used for basic thermal behavior prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation model with four free parameters specifically designed to accurately capture the heatbed's thermal inertia. Preferred for control optimization and predictive accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous time-series temperature measurements collected from the Physical Twin (PT) including internal and external sensor readings. Used for model calibration and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, AnomalyDetectionEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "External environmental data including ambient temperature and operational conditions affecting the incubator's thermal dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance OperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Control signals and actuator commands from the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) including heatbed and fan activation states."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuatorStateData, FanActuatorStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler, StateEstimationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker supporting AMQP protocol for asynchronous, decoupled communication between components (low-level driver, controller, and DT). Acts as central hub in star-based network topology."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataSynchronizationService
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms for dynamic parameter estimation that adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity, heat transfer coefficients) based on real-time sensor data to maintain model-physical twin alignment."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, PredictionService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithms generating optimal control strategies (e.g., PID tuning, adaptive controllers) by simulating system response to different actuator inputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlOptimizationService
]

instance AnomalyDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Monitoring algorithms identifying deviations from expected thermal behavior (e.g., sudden temperature spikes/drops) to trigger alerts for potential faults."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithms for real-time state estimation of the physical system using sensor data fusion and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance PythonScipyStack : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python and Scipy libraries used for model calibration, simulation, and optimization including parameter tuning for thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, ControlOptimizationService, PredictionService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring bidirectional synchronization between the Digital Twin and Physical Twin via RabbitMQ message broker. Handles real-time data exchange and actuator command propagation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSynchronizationInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service dynamically updating model parameters using real-time sensor data to ensure alignment between Digital Twin models and Physical Twin behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, CalibrationAction
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service employing calibrated simulation models to predict internal temperature trajectories under varying conditions including heatbed activation and ambient temperature changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalPredictionInsight, ControlRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating optimal control strategies by simulating system response to different actuator inputs and environmental disturbances."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalControlInsight, ActuatorAdjustmentAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service monitoring for deviations from expected thermal behavior and triggering alerts for potential faults such as sensor failures or insulation breaches."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously collecting and processing temperature data from sensors to enable real-time state estimation and situational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric heating element generating and distributing heat inside the insulated container. Limited by power supply and thermal capacity of the styrofoam box with overheating protection mechanisms."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the enclosed space. Limited by motor speed and airflow constraints with direct control from the cyber system."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller serving as the central cyber-physical interface for real-time monitoring and actuation. Implements closed-loop control using sensor feedback to adjust heatbed and fan."
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization status between Digital Twin and Physical Twin including data transmission frequencies and message queue states."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics of model-physical twin alignment including prediction errors and parameter confidence intervals."
]

instance ThermalPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted internal temperature trajectories under current and hypothetical conditions including thermal inertia effects captured by the four-parameter model."
]

instance OptimalControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal control parameters and strategies for maintaining target temperature including heatbed duty cycles and fan speed adjustments."
]

instance AnomalyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts for detected anomalies including temperature deviations, sensor failures, and potential insulation breaches with severity levels."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time state estimation of the physical system including current temperatures, actuator states, and environmental conditions."
]

instance CalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic recalibration of model parameters using real-time sensor data to maintain prediction accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of actuator setpoints (heatbed power, fan speed) based on control optimization results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for maintenance actions including sensor recalibration, insulation checks, and component replacements based on anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for control parameter adjustments including PID tuning and adaptive control strategies based on prediction results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Physical Components
instance StyrofoamInsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated container minimizing thermal losses and providing the physical environment for temperature regulation. Acts as the primary thermal mass in the system."
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the thermal incubator including ambient conditions and thermal dynamics."
    base:contains StyrofoamInsulatedContainer, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator,
        InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor,
        RaspberryPiController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT system is **indirect**, with the **Digital Twin (DT) acting as an observer** rather than a direct command issuer. The **controller** (implemented on a Raspberry Pi) receives **temperature regulation commands** (e.g., heating activation/deactivation, fan control) via the **RabbitMQ server** and executes them on the **Physical Twin (PT)**. The controller employs a **modified bang-bang control strategy**, where it issues **binary commands** (e.g., 'heatbed ON/OFF', 'fan ON/OFF') based on **reference temperature thresholds** (defined by **LL (Lower Limit)**, **UL (Upper Limit)**, **H (Heating Duration)**, and **C (Cooldown Duration)**). No explicit **validation/authentication** mechanisms are described for control messages, but the **low-level driver** enforces a **safe protocol** to prevent unsafe states (e.g., overheating). **Feedback systems** are implicit, as the controller relies on **periodic sensor updates** from the PT to adjust actions. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly addressed, but the **statechart-based controller** ensures deterministic behavior by enforcing sequential actuation and waiting periods. The DT does not directly issue commands; instead, it **monitors messages** between the controller and low-level driver to infer PT behavior."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator employs a **real-time synchronization mechanism** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart, relying on **time-series data collection** from sensors (e.g., two internal temperature sensors and one external sensor) to monitor the system's state continuously. The temporal granularity is dictated by the **sampling frequency of sensor data**, which is periodically read by the low-level driver to update the DT's models. The system incorporates **calibration algorithms** to dynamically adjust model parameters (e.g., Model B with four free parameters) based on observed discrepancies between the PT and the DT, ensuring alignment over time. Trade-offs between **model accuracy and computational speed** are addressed, as more accurate models (e.g., those capturing thermal inertia of the heatbed) may introduce higher latency in simulations. The DT also supports **what-if simulations** using co-simulations, which require real-time or near-real-time data relay from the PT to evaluate controller performance and optimize parameters. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **controller cycle time** and the need for timely anomaly detection via Kalman filters, though exact frequencies are not specified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **closed-loop architecture** (Document 1, Figure 1) where the **Physical Twin (PT)** and **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)** are bridged through **data collection**, **model calibration**, and **algorithm-driven decision-making**. Key components include:
  1. **Data Layer**: Sensors (two internal, one external temperature sensors) stream real-time data to a **time-series database** for visualization and analysis.
  2. **Model Layer**: Two simulation models (Model A with 2 free parameters, Model B with 4 free parameters) are calibrated dynamically using **tracking simulators** (Document 5) to adapt to changes in the PT’s behavior (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed).
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: A **Kalman filter** estimates non-measured states (e.g., heatbed temperature), while **co-simulation engines** run what-if scenarios to optimize controller parameters. The **self-adaptation loop** (Document 5) orchestrates this constellation by:
     - Detecting anomalies via the Kalman filter,
     - Scheduling experiments to gather new data,
     - Recalibrating Model B,
     - Updating the Kalman filter parameters,
     - Reconfiguring the controller based on simulation results.
  The system integrates **resource management** implicitly through the Raspberry Pi’s computational constraints and the trade-off between model fidelity and simulation speed (Document 1). No explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) or **service meshes** is provided, but the **centralized DT instance** acts as a unifying coordinator for all components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the incubator case study includes the following phases: **(1) System Definition and Hardware Setup** – Involves designing the Physical Twin (PT) with components such as an insulated styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, and temperature sensors (two internal, one external). **(2) Model Development** – Creation of abstract representations (e.g., differential equation-based dynamic models) at varying levels of abstraction, with calibration algorithms (e.g., parameter estimation) to refine models using real-time sensor data from the PT. **(3) Integration and Simulation** – Combining models, data, and algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) into a DT framework, enabling real-time monitoring and predictive simulations of the PT’s behavior under current operating conditions. **(4) Deployment and Monitoring** – The DT is deployed alongside the PT, using calibration algorithms to continuously update models with sensor data, ensuring alignment between the cyber and physical systems. **(5) Adaptive Optimization** – Monitors (e.g., safety and performance checks) detect deviations, triggering human or algorithmic interventions (e.g., controller adjustments) to maintain optimal PT operation. The lifecycle emphasizes **continuous evolution** through iterative model refinement and real-time data assimilation, with governance via structured transitions between phases (e.g., from model calibration to deployment)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity of the DT models** is evaluated through **experimental validation**, where the **4-parameter model** demonstrated superior accuracy in predicting the **thermal inertia of the heatbed** compared to the 2-parameter model. **Model calibration** is performed using real-time sensor data to adjust free parameters, ensuring alignment with the PT's behavior. **Uncertainty sources** include environmental factors (e.g., external temperature fluctuations) and model simplifications, with **error quantification** achieved via comparative analysis of simulated vs. observed temperature trajectories. The **validation process** relies on empirical testing (Section 4 of Document 2), where the DT's predictions are benchmarked against ground-truth sensor data. **Quality assurance** is implicit in the calibration loop, where model updates are triggered by discrepancies between the DT and PT. No explicit **verification standards**, **continuous monitoring protocols**, or **human-in-the-loop interventions** are detailed in the documents, though the DT's role in **safety** (e.g., preventing overheating) is inferred from its predictive and supervisory capabilities."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** for the incubator DT relies on a **RabbitMQ message broker**, deployed as a **centralized communication server** for synchronizing data and control signals between the **Physical Twin (PT)** and **Digital Twin (DT)** components. The infrastructure uses **message-based communication** (likely leveraging **AMQP protocol**, though not explicitly stated) to transmit **temperature sensor data**, **actuator commands**, and **control states**. The network topology is **star-based**, with the **Raspberry Pi (hosting the controller and low-level driver)** and **DT components** connecting to the RabbitMQ server. No explicit details on **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability mechanisms** (e.g., message persistence, clustering), or **security protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control) are provided in the documents. The system assumes **real-time or near-real-time communication** due to the control loop’s dependency on fresh sensor data."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The incubator DT is deployed in an **edge-centric architecture**, with the **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** serving as the primary hosting platform for the **controller**, **low-level driver**, and **RabbitMQ server**. The system requires **minimal computational resources**, as it runs lightweight components (e.g., bang-bang controller, message broker) and **simulation models** (thermal dynamics with 2–4 free parameters) for predictive analysis. No explicit **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., cloud offloading, distributed computing) or **fault tolerance** features (e.g., redundancy, failover) are described. The deployment model is **monolithic-on-edge**, with all critical components co-located on the Raspberry Pi to minimize latency. **Availability/reliability** is ensured through the **safe protocol** in the low-level driver and the **deterministic control logic**, but no formal redundancy or backup systems are mentioned."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its **monitoring and control mechanisms**, as depicted in the schematic overview (Fig. 1). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation for reliable operation. **Monitors** within the DT system check for safe and optimal performance, detecting violations that may trigger corrective actions (e.g., adjustments to the physical twin or algorithmic interventions). The system leverages **real-time data** from sensors (two internal and one external temperature sensors) to maintain operational integrity. While explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, access control, or threat mitigation) are not detailed in the provided documents, the DT framework inherently supports **fail-safe mechanisms** by integrating **supervisory controllers** and **state estimators** to ensure system robustness. The incubator’s hardware (Raspberry Pi-based controller) and software (open-source repository) imply adherence to **industry-standard safety practices** for embedded systems, though specific compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508) are not referenced."
]
